title: Rearranging digits to get a multiple of 5

content.problemStatement:
Given a positive integer NNN, MoEngage wants you to determine if it is possible to rearrange the digits of NNN (in decimal representation) and obtain a multiple of 555.

For example, when N=108N = 108N=108, we can rearrange its digits to construct 180=36⋅5180 = 36 \cdot 5180=36⋅5 which is a multiple of 555.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. The description of the TTT test cases follow.
Each test case consists of two lines
The first line contains a single integer DDD, the number of digits in NNN.
The second line consists of a string of length DDD, the number NNN (in decimal representation). It is guaranteed that the string does not contain leading zeroes and consists only of the characters 0,1,…90, 1, \dots 90,1,…9.


Output Format

For each test case, print Yes\texttt{Yes}Yes if it is possible to rearrange the digits of NNN so that it becomes a multiple 555. Otherwise, print No\texttt{No}No.

You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings YeS\texttt{YeS}YeS, yEs\texttt{yEs}yEs, yes\texttt{yes}yes and YES\texttt{YES}YES will all be treated as identical).
constraints: Sum of D over all test cases <= 1000
N consists only of characters '0'–'9' and has no leading zero
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer D, the number of digits of N. The second line contains a string of length D representing N (no leading zeros).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if some permutation of the digits of N is divisible by 5, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: digit 5 exists → Yes. Test case 2: digit 0 exists → Yes. Test case 3: digits are 1 and 9 only → No.
First number has no 0 or 5 → No. Second number is 5 itself → Yes.
Contains digit 0 (and 5) → we can arrange as 500, 050, etc., which are multiples of 5.

hidden.label: general case without zero or five

solution.approach: A number is divisible by 5 iff its last digit is 0 or 5. When we can permute the digits arbitrarily, we only need to know whether the multiset of digits contains at least one '0' or one '5'. If such a digit exists we can place it at the end and put any non‑zero digit at the front (or the digit itself if the number consists of a single digit). Therefore the algorithm is: for each test case scan the string; if a character is '0' or '5' output Yes, otherwise No.
solution.timeComplexity: O(D) per test case, where D is the number of digits of the current N. Because the total sum of D over all test cases is ≤1000, the overall complexity is O(1000).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input string.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        D = int(next(it))
        s = next(it)
        out.append('Yes' if ('0' in s or '5' in s) else 'No')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
